scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Spec Conflicts with Existing Behavior
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) existing premium/gold tier behavior identified as potentially impacted with specific rates named (20% and 15%), (2) escalation specifies what would change — premium/gold customers would lose their discounts under naive replacement, (3) clarifying questions cover all three dimensions (replace entirely, layer on top, precedence), (4) no implementation that silently drops tier discounts, (5) no implementation provided at all — escalation blocks all three ACs pending clarification.

observations.text: Correctly identified the core conflict: spec is silent on premium and gold tiers
Named specific discount rates (20% premium, 15% gold) that would be lost
Refused to implement anything rather than risk a silent regression
Escalation question cleanly covers all three resolution paths (replace, layer, precedence)
Low confidence level (30) appropriately reflects the ambiguity

Escalation asks two questions in one rather than three distinctly numbered questions — the replace/retire and layer/precedence options are combined, making it slightly less precise than the ideal three-question format
